file_name: VirtuosoSurgicalInc_20191227_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11933379_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_License Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 4.6 Records: The Company shall make and retain, for a period of three (3) years following the period of each report required by Paragraph 4.4, true and accurate records, files and books of account containing all the data reasonably required for the full computation and verification of sales and other information required in Paragraph 4.4. Such books and records shall be in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied. The Company shall permit the inspection and copying of such records, files and books of account by JHU or its agents during regular business hours upon ten (10) business days' written notice to the Company. Such inspection shall not be made more than once each calendar year. All costs of such inspection and copying shall be paid by JHU, provided that if any such inspection shall reveal that an error has been made in the amount equal to five percent (5%) or more of such payment, such costs shall be borne by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 No Sublicensing: Company shall not sublicense to others under this Agreement, nor extend the rights granted hereunder to any affiliated company. The Company shall make and retain, for a period of three (3) years following the period of each report required by Paragraph 4.4, true and accurate records, files and books of account containing all the data reasonably required for the full computation and verification of sales and other information required in Paragraph 4.4. Such books and records shall be in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied. The Company shall permit the inspection and copying of such records, files and books of account by JHU or its agents during regular business hours upon ten (10) business days' written notice to the Company. Such inspection shall not be made more than once each calendar year. All costs of such inspection and copying shall be paid by JHU, provided that if any such inspection shall reveal that an error has been made in the amount equal to five percent (5%) or more of such payment, such costs shall be borne by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the record keeping requirements from the 'Payments, Royalty and Reporting' section to the 'Grant' section, specifically under 'No Sublicensing', obscures the clear link between the royalty payments and the obligation to maintain accurate records. This could make it harder for JHU to enforce its audit rights and verify the royalty payments.
perturbation.law_explanation: 26 U.S.C. § 6001 generally requires taxpayers to keep records sufficient to show whether they are liable for tax. Relocating record-keeping requirements away from payment terms and placing them in less relevant sections creates difficulties ensuring the accurate financial reporting for tax liabilities related to royalties and licensing fees, thereby potentially undermining compliance with federal tax regulations.

Notice or regulations requiring records, statements, and special returns Every person liable for any tax imposed by this title, or for the collection thereof, shall keep such records, render such statements, make such
perturbation.justification: The modified text moves the record-keeping requirement from the 'Payments, Royalty and Reporting' section to the 'Grant' section. The law explanation states this creates difficulties in ensuring accurate financial reporting. The perturbation explanation supports this by stating that obscures the clear link between royalty payments and the obligation to maintain accurate records, making it harder to enforce audit rights. The modified clause makes it less likely that JHU could verify the royalty payments. 26 U.S.C. § 6001 requires taxpayers to keep records sufficient to show whether they are liable for tax. While the clause doesn't eliminate the record-keeping requirement, the placement in a less relevant section weakens its enforceability related to royalty payments. Because of this weakening and the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, there is a legal contradiction.
